title: Astracantha lateritians
intro: Astracantha lateritius, also known as Alhagi camelorum, is a perennial plant species native to the Middle East and Central Asia, characterized by its thorny stems and small white or pink flowers. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, including its potential to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and lower blood sugar levels, making it a useful remedy for various health conditions. The bioactive constituents of Astracantha lateritius include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Astracantha lateritius is commonly used in traditional medicine and is prepared in various herbal forms, such as teas, infusions, and capsules, as well as in combination with other herbs to enhance its health benefits.
